findings.title: No script computes FasterVQA, a metric reported for all 7 datasets in Table 2
findings.quote: > **TODO:** Add metric computation scripts for FasterVQA, DOVER, and $E^*_{warp}$.
findings.claim: FasterVQA is reported in Table 2 (every dataset) and Fig. 1, but no Python file in the repo computes it; the README itself lists FasterVQA computation as an unfinished TODO. A repo-wide search for 'faster' in .py files returns no metric code (only this README line).
findings.concern: A headline metric appearing in every row of the main quantitative table has no computation path in the released code, so those values cannot be reproduced from the repo.
findings.resolution: Authors: release the FasterVQA evaluation script (model weights, preprocessing, and call site) used to produce the Table 2 / Fig. 1 numbers.
findings.reason: README.md line 269 contains the exact TODO quote and no FasterVQA computation script exists anywhere in the repo.

findings.title: E*warp script imports an 'ewarp' module that does not exist in the repo
findings.quote:
original_dir = os.getcwd()
raft_dir = os.path.join(original_dir, "RAFT")
os.chdir(raft_dir)
from ewarp import Ewarp as Ewarp
results, avg_score = Ewarp(args)
findings.claim: eval_ewarp.py chdir's to ./RAFT (i.e. finetune/scripts/RAFT) and does `from ewarp import Ewarp`, but no `ewarp.py` exists anywhere in the repo, the `finetune/scripts/RAFT` directory does not exist (RAFT lives at finetune/utils/RAFT), and the default `--model finetune/scripts/models/raft-things.pth` path is also absent (the checkpoint is at finetune/utils/RAFT/raft-things.pth).
findings.concern: E*warp is reported for every dataset in Table 2 and in Fig. 1, but the only script that would compute it cannot run because the module that actually computes the value (Ewarp) is missing and the RAFT directory/model paths it assumes do not exist.
findings.resolution: Authors: add the `ewarp.py` (Ewarp implementation) and fix the RAFT directory / checkpoint paths, so E*warp in Table 2 / Fig. 1 is reproducible.
findings.reason: Lines 146-150 of eval_ewarp.py match the quote exactly; ewarp.py is absent from the entire repo; finetune/scripts/RAFT does not exist (RAFT is at finetune/utils/RAFT); default model path finetune/scripts/models/raft-things.pth is also wrong.

findings.title: DOVER metric depends on an external 'DOVER' package absent from repo and requirements
findings.quote:
from DOVER.evaluate_a_set_of_videos import evaluate_set as dover

input_path = os.path.abspath(input_path)

dover_results = dover(input_path, device=device)
findings.claim: eval_dover.py imports `DOVER.evaluate_a_set_of_videos`, but there is no `DOVER` package/directory in the repo and `DOVER` is not listed in requirements.txt; the import will fail at runtime.
findings.concern: DOVER is a headline VQA metric in Table 2 (all datasets), Table 3, and Fig. 1, yet the script that would compute it cannot import its core dependency from the released code, so the DOVER values are not reproducible from the repo alone.
findings.resolution: Authors: vendor or pin the exact DOVER implementation (and its weights/config) used, or add install/setup instructions to requirements/README so DOVER eval is runnable.
findings.reason: eval_dover.py line 150 matches the quote exactly; no DOVER directory or package exists in the repo and DOVER is absent from requirements.txt.

findings.title: inference.sh evaluates every dataset against UDM10 ground truth
findings.claim: The reproduction driver inference.sh hardcodes `--gt datasets/test/UDM10/GT` in the eval step for SPMCS, YouHQ40, RealVSR, MVSR4x, and VideoLQ (only the UDM10 block uses the matching GT). The shown SPMCS block evaluates SPMCS predictions against UDM10 ground truth.
findings.concern: Anyone running the provided script to reproduce Table 2 computes full-reference metrics (PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/DISTS) for 5 of 6 datasets against the wrong ground truth; eval_metrics.py skips predictions whose filenames are absent from the UDM10 GT folder (eval_metrics.py:174), so those datasets' fidelity numbers come out empty or against mismatched clips.
findings.resolution: Fix each eval block to use the dataset's own GT (e.g. `--gt datasets/test/SPMCS/GT`); confirm whether the Table 2 numbers were produced with corrected paths.
findings.reason: inference.sh lines 22-25 match the quote exactly and inspection confirms all five non-UDM10 eval blocks hardcode --gt datasets/test/UDM10/GT.

findings.title: eval_dover.py assigns DOVER scores with an off-by-one index (i-1)
findings.quote:
for i, name in enumerate(pred_names):
    results[name] = dover_results[i-1]
findings.claim: Per-video DOVER scores are mapped to filenames with `dover_results[i-1]`, so the first sorted name (i=0) receives `dover_results[-1]` (the last video's score) and every name is shifted by one relative to `dover_results`.
findings.concern: Per-sample DOVER values are misattributed by one position; this does not change the mean (a cyclic permutation), so the averaged Table-2/Table-3 DOVER number is unaffected, but per-sample DOVER outputs are wrong and any per-clip analysis would be misleading.
findings.resolution: Use `results[name] = dover_results[i]` and confirm whether `evaluate_set` returns scores in the same sorted order as `pred_names`.
findings.reason: eval_dover.py lines 158-159 exactly match the quote; the i-1 index is confirmed and causes a cyclic permutation of per-sample scores.

findings.title: Shipped accelerate config uses 2 GPUs / effective batch 4, vs paper's 4 GPUs / batch 8
findings.quote:
gpu_ids: "0, 1"
num_processes: 2  # should be the same as the number of GPUs
findings.claim: The released accelerate config launches 2 processes (2 GPUs) and both train shell scripts set `--batch_size 2 --gradient_accumulation_steps 1`, giving a global batch size of 4. The paper (§4.1) states training used '4 NVIDIA A800-80G GPUs with the total batch size 8'.
findings.concern: The shipped configuration does not match the paper's reported training setup (batch size 4 vs 8 / 2 GPUs vs 4); reproducing with the default files would not match the paper's effective batch size, which can affect the trained weights.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm the GPU count / batch configuration used for the released checkpoints, or update accelerate_config.yaml / the shell scripts to the paper's 4-GPU, batch-8 setting.
findings.paper_ref: §4.1 Implementation Details ('4 NVIDIA A800-80G GPUs with the total batch size 8')
findings.reason: accelerate_config.yaml lines 3-4 match the quote exactly; train scripts confirm --batch_size 2 --gradient_accumulation_steps 1 on 2 GPUs (effective batch 4); paper_text.txt line 609 confirms '4 NVIDIA A800-80G GPUs with the total batch size 8'.
